stories.label: Who handed over Billy Bishop?
stories.caption: Yes votes expanded provincial control over Billy Bishop. Ministers who voted Yes are outlined.

stories.label: Who buried FOI?
stories.caption: Yes votes weakened transparency. Look for cabinet Yes votes and caucus rebels.

stories.label: Who backed special economic zones?
stories.caption: Yes votes concentrated land-use power. Soft seats that voted Yes are prime organizing targets.

stories.label: Soft seats that voted Yes
stories.caption: Close 2025 races where the MPP still voted Yes — high-leverage pressure targets.

stories.label: Government-line scorecard
stories.caption: How often each MPP voted Yes on OAC’s watched bills — a simple accountability heat map.

stories.label: Follow the expense outliers
stories.caption: Disclosed travel, meals, hospitality. Pair with a Yes vote to ask harder questions.

billBlurbs.Bill 110: Removes Toronto from the airport’s tripartite agreement and expands provincial control over Billy Bishop.
billBlurbs.Bill 97: Centralizes authority while weakening freedom-of-information and oversight.
billBlurbs.Bill 5: Creates special economic zone powers that can override local planning.
billBlurbs.Bill 17: “Building Faster and Smarter” law — speeds housing, transit, and infrastructure by rewriting municipal planning and development-charge rules.
billBlurbs.Bill 60: “Fighting Delays, Building Faster” omnibus — expands ministerial planning power, transit overrides, and other delay-cutting measures.
billBlurbs.Bill 24: Budget measures act — implements the government’s fiscal agenda by amending multiple statutes in one package.
billBlurbs.Bill 68: Second budget-measures package — more statute changes to lock in government budget and “Protect Ontario” priorities.
